sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: Analog tape model based on the Jiles-Atherton magnetic hysteresis model. Port of AnalogTapeModel by Jatin Chowdhury.

sections.heading: Drive
sections.body: Controls the input level into the hysteresis model. Higher values push the tape harder, increasing harmonic saturation and compression.

sections.heading: Saturation
sections.body: Controls the magnetic saturation of the virtual tape. Lower values increase saturation for a thicker, more compressed sound.

sections.heading: Bias
sections.body: Controls the tape bias / width parameter. Affects the reversibility of the magnetization process, changing the harmonic character of the distortion.

sections.heading: Tone
sections.body: Tilt EQ applied before the tape stage. Center (0.5) is flat. Turn left for darker, warmer sound; turn right for brighter, more present sound.

sections.heading: Speed
sections.body: Simulates tape speed from slow to fast. Slower speeds roll off more high frequencies (like real tape) and shift the head bump resonance lower.

sections.heading: Flutter
sections.body: Combined wow and flutter effect. Adds slow pitch drift (wow) and faster pitch modulation (flutter) through a modulated delay line. Includes Ornstein-Uhlenbeck variance for realistic irregularity.

sections.heading: Chew
sections.body: Simulates physical tape damage. Creates random periods of signal dropout and lowpass filtering, as if the tape has been crinkled or chewed.

sections.heading: Mix
sections.body: Blends between the dry (unprocessed) and wet (tape-processed) signal. Useful for parallel processing.

sections.heading: Credits
sections.body: Based on AnalogTapeModel (CHOW Tape) by Jatin Chowdhury. Hysteresis model from the DAFx paper: https://ccrma.stanford.edu/~jatin/420/tape/TapeModel_DAFx.pdf
